key_insight: Increasing cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar raises copper particle velocity above the bonding threshold, producing denser, more ductile deposits with better conductivity.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies higher spray pressure as a practical control knob for balancing conductivity, ductility, and strength in copper CSAM parts.
method: LightSPEE3D cold spray copper deposits made at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar
Measured microstructure and phase evolution with XRD and SEM
Evaluated hardness, eddy-current conductivity, and tensile response
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic increases in particle velocity, XRD-driven refinement and dislocation changes, higher conductivity, and a clear ductility gain at higher pressures, with fully ductile fracture at the best conditions.
watch_item: Higher pressure improved multifunctional performance, but hardness decreased due to recovery/recrystallisation, so process settings must be tuned to avoid trading away wear resistance.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
